preprocessing: Drop any columns identified in the EDA as irrelevant or high in missing values
Impute missing values in numerical columns using median or mean, as appropriate per EDA recommendations
Impute missing values in categorical columns with the most frequent value if needed
Encode categorical columns as indicated by the EDA using OneHotEncoder or label encoding as appropriate
Convert the 'Churn' target column to binary values if it is not already (e.g., map 'Yes'/'No' to 1/0)
Handle class imbalance in the 'Churn' target using SMOTE, class weights, or undersampling/oversampling as suggested by the EDA
Scale numerical features using StandardScaler or MinMaxScaler if required by the EDA
Split the dataset into training and testing sets with stratification on 'Churn'
training: Fit the classifier on the training data
Evaluate the fitted model on the test set using accuracy, precision, recall, or the metric identified as most relevant for churn in the consultation or EDA
